observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to reconcile their records, present a breakdown of the P40,738,778.52 discrepancy, and submit the following for verification:
Municipal Treasurer:
Cashbook for Cash-in-Bank
Cashbook for Cash Advances
Cashbook for Cash-in-Treasury
Municipal Accountant:
Bank Reconciliation Statements for December 2024 (GF)
Statement of Cash Flows for CY 2024
Notes to the Financial Statements for CY 2024
General and Subsidiary Ledgers

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to:
Promptly submit DVs and supporting documents to the Accounting Office for initial recording upon grant of cash advances;
Upon liquidation, immediately submit the Report of Disbursements along with duly accomplished vouchers/payrolls and supporting documents to the Accountant within the prescribed period; and
Submit the cashbooks to the Audit Team for audit.
Require the Municipal Accountant to submit the Schedule of Cash Advances and Subsidiary Ledgers.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasurer to submit the Report of Collections and Deposits, along with the official receipts and validated deposit slips, to the Municipal Accountant for proper recording, and then forward the same to the Audit Team for review and validation.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Direct the Inventory Committee to:
Create an inventory plan;
Execute the one-time cleansing of the Municipality's PPE accounts following the prescribed procedures outlined in COA Circular No. 2020-006; and
Ensure that the inventory taking is conducted in the presence of the representative(s) from the Commission of Audit.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to review the journal entry vouchers affecting “Due from Other Funds” and “Due to Other Funds” accounts to identify the causes of the discrepancies and prepare the necessary adjustments to reflect the correct balances. Thereafter, periodic reconciliation of the accounts should be made to ensure that all transactions are properly and accurately recorded in the books of accounts.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) Secretariat, BAC Chairman, and Municipal Engineer to furnish the COA Office with a copy of perfected contracts and their supporting documents following the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001 for the timely review and validation of the same.

We recommended that the Chief Local Executive direct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) Secretariat and BAC Chairman to send written invitations or notices to the Commission on Audit at least five (5) calendar days before the scheduled public biddings, as required by the Revised IRR of RA 9184.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to maintain separate cashbooks for the 20% Development Fund; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain a separate set of accounting records and financial statements intended for this fund.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) Secretariat, the BAC Chairman, Municipal Engineer, and GAD Focal Person to furnish this Office with copies of the perfected contracts and their supporting documents, along with the bidding documents, and necessary supporting documentation for the disbursement vouchers.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasurer, Municipal Accountant, and the Trust Fund administrator (if designated) to coordinate with each other to facilitate the submission of the reports and financial transaction documents pertaining to the Trust Fund for review.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Trust Fund administrator (if designated), Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer, BAC Chairman, Municipal Engineer to coordinate with each other to facilitate the submission of the memorandum of agreement, contract, bidding documents, and financial transaction reports and documents pertaining to the Seal of Good Local Governance (SGLG) Incentive Fund for review.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Affix her signature in the following payroll from January to December 2024 to signify approval:
Initial General Payroll – for the granting of cash advance
Approved Payroll – for the liquidation of cash advances
Require the Municipal Treasurer and department heads concerned, as authorized approving signatories, to affix their signatures on both the initial and approved payrolls from January to December 2024;
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to:
Submit the following, in compliance with the proper documentation in the grant and liquidation of cash advances per COA Circular No. 2012-001:
For the Granting of Cash Advances:
Certification from the Chief Accountant that previous cash advances have been fully liquidated and accounted for in the books
For the Liquidation of Cash Advances:
Report of Disbursements certified correct by the accountable officer
Follow-up the Authorized Government Depository Bank (AGDB) for the approval of the Automated Teller Machine (ATM) payroll system to minimize cash advances in the payment of salaries and wages of government officials and employees.
Require all concerned LGU personnel to submit their DTRs for January to December 2024, and remind them that DTRs should be submitted monthly as supporting documents for the monthly payroll;
Require the casual/contractual personnel to submit duly approved Monthly Accomplishment Reports for January to December 2024 to support their claims indicated in the payroll;
Direct the Municipal Accountant to review and ensure that the following are complied with before recording 100% liquidation in the JEV and finally forwarded to the COA Audit Team:
The following documentary requirements are attached to the DVs:
For the Granting of Cash Advances:
Certification from the Chief Accountant that previous cash advances have been fully liquidated and accounted for in the books
Initial general payroll or list of payees indicating their net pay
For the Liquidation of Cash Advances:
Report of Disbursements certified correct by the accountable officer
Approved payrolls/vouchers duly acknowledged/signed by the payee/s
Duly signed Daily Time Records (January-December 2024)
Duly Approved Monthly Accomplishment Reports (for casual/contractual personnel)
The payrolls and DVs are duly approved by the authorized approving signatories and duly signed by the claimants.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasurer to ensure that all collections are remitted and deposited within the prescribed period as required by the laws, rules, and regulations to prevent possible misappropriation, theft, or loss of funds.

Direct the Municipal Treasurer to submit copies of checks for CY 2024, along with the CY 2024 Report of Checks Issued, including canceled checks, to the Municipal Accountant for recording and reconciliation. The Municipal Accountant shall then forward them to the Office of the Auditor for audit review; and
Ensure that, for future transactions starting in January 2025, copies of checks are attached to the disbursement vouchers, and the corresponding RCI is submitted to the Accounting Office, along with the completed vouchers and supporting documents, at the end of each month for timely recording and reconciliation

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the BAC Secretariat to submit all perfected contracts and purchase orders to this Office within the prescribed period for timely review and evaluation.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant to reconcile, prepare, and submit a Reconciliation of Actual Amounts on a Comparable Basis and Actual Amounts in the Financial Statements.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Treasurer to have the DVs and supporting documents stamped 'PAID' after payment has been made, in order to prevent the possible re-use of these documents in similar transactions, thereby safeguarding government funds against unnecessary loss through double payments.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
Issue a designation order to properly designate collecting officers to help the Municipal Treasurer in the municipal collections;
Require all designated accountable officers to apply with the Bureau of Treasury a bond sufficient to cover his/her maximum cash accountability. Management should determine the maximum cash accountability to be assigned to the accountable officer before the application with the Bureau of Treasury; and
Allot in the annual budget additional fidelity bond for the municipal collectors.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
Direct the Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant and General Services Officer with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects;
Direct the General Services Officer to:
Maintain the Local Road Inventory and Road Map, and to keep a complete Local Road Network Property Cards (LRNPCs) for each local road and its components; and
Render a Report on Local Road Network (RLRN) of the LGU concerned at the end of the accounting period – Annex A.
Direct the Inventory Committee to conduct an annual physical count of the road networks and prepare the Report on the Physical Count of the Local Road Networks (RPCLRN) – Annex C; and
Require the Municipal Accountant to:
Keep and maintain subsidiary records for roads and road components for every road network and prepare a lapsing schedule for the computation of the depreciation for each component at the end of the year; and
Disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements the total road network system following the format provided in Annex B of COA Circular No. 2015-008.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
Require the GSO to:
Maintain PPE Property Cards (PC) to account for the receipt and disposition;
Ensure that the deliveries of properties and equipment are immediately recorded in the Property Cards (PC) based on the AIR and other supporting documents.
Direct the Municipal Accountant to:
Make reconciliation with inventory reports, property cards, and subsidiary ledgers;
Ensure that the deliveries of properties and equipment are immediately recorded in the PPE Subsidiary Ledger Cards based on the AIR and other supporting documents; and
Ensure strict adherence to the above-stated laws, rules, and regulations.

We have recommended to the Municipal Accountant to transfer the identified PPE accounts from the Trust Fund to the General Fund books and take up the depreciation expenses of these accounts, except land, from the date of recognition to the current year so that the correct balances will be reflected in the financial statements.  Depreciation for the prior years that lapsed should be charged to Prior Year’s Adjustment separate from the current year’s depreciation expenses.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to direct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) Chairman to:
Comply with the regulations and furnish the COA Office with the copy of perfected contracts together with their supporting documents in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001 for the timely review, evaluation and inspection of the same; and
Send written invitations or notices to the COA Office and at least two other qualified observers at least five (5) calendar days before the scheduled public biddings as required in the Revised IRR of RA 9184.

We have recommended to the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to effect the transfer of the unexpended balances of the QRF and the DRRMF-MOOE to the Special Trust Fund and revert the unexpended balances of 2015 and 2016 to the General Fund.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
Direct the LDRRM Officer to update and incorporate in the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) under a separate caption the list of projects and activities to be funded from the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years to strengthen the Municipality’s capacity for disaster risk reduction and management and enhance its disaster preparedness and response capabilities; and
Ensure that all budgeted projects, programs, and activities outlined in the LDRRMFIP are implemented accordingly in optimal, maximum, and effective use.

We have recommended to the Municipal Accountant to make necessary adjustments for the reclassification of the expenses that were inappropriately recorded in the Food Supplies Expenses account for the fair presentation of account balances in the financial statements, provided there were funds appropriated for the purpose.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
Direct the Municipal Budget Officer to:
Refrain from using the “Discretionary Fund” account since it is not in the Revised Chart of Accounts per PPSAS and COA Circular No. 2015-009. Appropriation for the discretionary purposes of the Local Chief Executive shall be provided using the Extraordinary and Miscellaneous Expenses (EME) account, which shall not exceed two percent (2%) of the actual receipts derived from basic real property tax in the next preceding calendar year and that no other amount shall be appropriated for the same purpose;
Appropriate the budget for the expenses not falling under the description of EME to a more appropriate account(s); and
Direct the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustments to reclassify the various expenses charged against and recorded in the Extraordinary and Miscellaneous Expenses (EME) account that do not fall within its description for the financial statements to be presented fairly.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to direct�the Municipal Budget Officer to ensure that, before signing and forwarding the same to the accounting department, the Obligation Requests are:
Prepared in accordance with the instructions supplied in Annex A of COA Circular No. 2006-002 dated January 31, 2006;
Chronologically numbered following the format provided in Annex A; and
Properly and completely accomplished indicating all the vital information on its face, including the date, responsibility center, F.P.P., and account code.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
Urge the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to observe the prevailing policies as prescribed in Sections 70 and 71 Volume I of the Manual on NGAS for LGU and COA Circular No. 2005-001 dated February 28, 2005 as restated with amendments by COA Circular No. 2006-002 dated January 31, 2006; and
Require the Municipal Budget Officer to submit not later than the twentieth day after the end of the month the Status of Appropriations, Allotments and Obligations (SAOOB) for both the current and continuing appropriations.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Accountant to:
Maintain and update the journals and general and subsidiary ledgers for the account balances reported in the financial statements under the trust fund and submit the same to the auditor in support of the latter in order to facilitate the timely conduct of the audit and prompt reconciliation of accounts; and
Prepare the Statement of Cash Flows using the Direct Method showing the cash receipts and cash payments made in accordance with the standards to enhance the transparency and accountability of financial reports.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to ensure that the payroll deductions shall in no case reduce the employees' monthly net take-home pay to an amount lower than�Five Thousand Pesos (₱5,000.00), and strictly comply with the provisions stated in�Section 49 of the General Appropriations Act for Fiscal Year 2021.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to direct the Chairman of the Solid Waste Management Board to facilitate and ensure the fast approval of the agency’s Solid Waste Management Plan and copy furnish the Audit Team of the said plan once approved.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:

Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit promptly the bank reconciliation statements monthly for all the funds of the Municipality and to prepare the necessary adjusting entries for bank charges, interest income, and staled checks to bring back the cash in bank account in its corrected balance;

Authorize the Municipal Accountant to enroll in e-access of their accounts so that bank statements could readily be available at any time; and

Augment the workforce of the Accounting Office to facilitate the timely recording and preparation of financial reports.

Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit promptly the bank reconciliation statements monthly for all the funds of the Municipality and to prepare the necessary adjusting entries for bank charges, interest income, and staled checks to bring back the cash in bank account in its corrected balance;

Augment the workforce of the Accounting Office to facilitate the timely recording and preparation of financial reports.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to require the Municipal Accountant to:
Compute the depreciation of all depreciable properties including those under the Trust Fund, and any additional acquisitions of depreciable assets;
Make the necessary adjustments in the books based on the computation, and provide yearly depreciation for all depreciable properties following the above-mentioned rules and regulations; and
Conduct assessment regularly whether there are changes in the estimated useful life or the expected pattern of consumption of those future economic benefits or service potential of the remaining un-depreciated portion of a PPE and revised when the appropriateness of a change can be demonstrated.

Direct the RHU to conduct an inventory count on the remaining stocks of medicines and vitamins and other consumable medical supplies at the end of the year and submit inventory report to the Accounting Department for the preparation of the necessary adjusting entries to the inventory and expense accounts affected;
Direct the Municipal Budget Officer to use the proper budgetary accounts in charging the expenditures of the Municipality and augment the accounts, if necessary; and
Direct the Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant to strictly follow COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 01, 2015, in the preparation of the Budget Plan and the recording of transactions of the Municipality, respectively.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
To make necessary adjustments for the reclassification of the expenses that were inappropriately recorded in the Food Supplies Expenses account for the fair presentation of account balances in the financial statements; and in the future transactions, to appropriately classify the expenses and properly accomplish the Journal Entry Vouchers (JEVs).
To record separately the expenditures incurred for the purchases of relief goods intended for distribution to families affected by the COVID-19 pandemic in the Welfare Goods Expenses account.
To record the expenses related to meals and snacks during the assemblies, activities, and programs of the Municipality to a more proper financial account.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
Direct the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant to include in the next Budget Plan an appropriation for the Environmental/Sanitary Services for the contract of services for the environmental upkeep and sanitation of the Municipality;
Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and properly accomplish the Journal Entry Vouchers (JEVs) to demonstrate the proper accounting and recording of the transactions; and
Direct the Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant to strictly follow COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 01, 2015, in the preparation of the Budget Plan and recording of the transactions of the Municipality, respectively.

Transfer the completed projects recorded in the PPE accounts under the Trust Fund to the General Fund books and take up the depreciation of these accounts from the prior years to the current year so that the correct balances will be reflected in the financial statements.  Depreciation for the prior years that lapsed should be charged to Prior Year’s Adjustment separate from the current year depreciation expenses;
Adhere to the prescribed accounting rules relative to the asset recognition under Property, Plant and Equipment, including maintenance of Construction in Progress (CIP) account for the ongoing and incomplete construction/infrastructure projects; and
Provide necessary adjusting entries to recognize the completed infrastructure project of the Mamaanun-Angayen Road to the proper PPE account and transfer the same to the General Fund books.

Secure the correct cash book form and establish the Cashbooks as Collecting/Liquidating and Cash Disbursing Officer; and
Keep an updated cashbook which shall be made available to the management for easy monitoring and reporting and to the COA in the conduct of the audit of cash and accounts of the accountable officer.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the collecting officer should remit her collections intact daily or in a regular basis to the Municipal Treasurer, who should in turn deposit in the authorized government depository bank immediately or as often as necessary to forestall losses through theft or misappropriation.
Likewise, we recommended the Municipal Treasurer to strictly require the Collecting Officer to remit her collections intact daily or the next working day. When collections are minimal and daily deposit thereof becomes costly and impractical, he shall deposit her collection at least once a week, or as soon as the collections reach ₱10,000.00 pursuant to Item 32, Chapter II of Revised Cash Examination Manual.

We recommended the following:
The Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit promptly the bank reconciliation statements monthly for all the funds of the Municipality and to prepare the necessary adjusting entries for bank charges, interest income and staled checks to bring back the cash in bank account in its corrected balance.
The Local Chief Executive and the Municipal Treasurer as signatories of the bank accounts of the Municipality to authorize the Municipal Accountant to enroll on e-access of their accounts so that bank statements could readily be available at any time.
The Municipal Accountant to request from the Local Chief Executive additional personnel in the Accounting Office to augment the workforce to facilitate the timely recording and preparation of financial reports.

We recommended that the General Services Officer should submit Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption of government motor transportation; and strictly comply with the provisions of the Manual on Audit of Fuel Consumption of Government Motor Vehicles as prescribed under COA Circular No. 77-61 to be properly guided in the documentation of fuel consumption and in the use of government vehicles/equipment as well as to be able to regulate fuel consumption.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer should see to it that before payment for the salary of the employees/personnel is made, a Daily Time Record (DTR) and its attachments should be submitted first.
We recommended that Bid and Award Committee should prepare the Purchase Orders completely and with relevant information enumerated under Item B of COA Circular 96-010 dated August 15, 1996.

We recommended to the BAC to:
Send written invitations or notices to the mandated observers, especially COA, at least five (5) calendar days before the scheduled public biddings as provided in the IRR of RA 9184; and
Submit all government contracts and its supporting documents to the Auditor’s Office within five (5) days from the execution of a contract in accordance with COA Circular No 2009-001.

Management to attach duly accomplished Acceptance and Inspection Report to the Disbursement Voucher.

And to prepare a letter informing the servicing bank of the wrong postings they made and inquire or secure the debit memos to validate those amounts debited by the bank without any corresponding entry in the books.
We have recommended to the Municipal Mayor and Municipal Treasurer to authorize the Municipal Accountant to enroll in online access of their accounts so that bank statements could readily be available at any time.

We have recommended that the management should give emphasis on the collection of the delinquent real property taxes by applying the administrative remedies against delinquent taxpayers as provided for under the Local Government Code.  We have further recommend that management to hire additional employee assigned in the office of the assessor, otherwise, require the concerned employee to render overtime in order to prepare and submit the estimated real property tax collectibles.

We have recommended to the Management to create an appraisal / inventory committee to conduct actual physical inventory taking of all properties, plant and equipment.  Also instruct the Municipal Accountant to make necessary adjustments on the records deficiencies noted in the course of inventory taking and in the reconciliation of the inventory report with the subsidiary ledgers. After physical count and report of PPE’s are completed, prepare and maintain a subsidiary ledger card on each equipment and should bear a Property Number. The municipal accountant to provide residual value in computing the depreciation.
We also have recommended to the Administrative Officer to update the list of properties that needs to be insured and submit the same to GSIS. This will guarantee that all properties are adequately covered by insurance in order to protect the interest of the government. For lost / damaged properties without covering insurance, liability therefore should devolve upon the parties responsible.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to issue designation order for the designation of a collecting officer and to allot in the annual budget additional fidelity bond for the municipal collectors and to post a fidelity bond to the Bureau of Treasury. If warranted, after the election, the new Municipal Mayor to appoint permanent employees who will do the collection of taxes and monies for the municipality.
observations.managementAction: For CY 2017, it was already included in the CY 2017 Budget the fidelity bond of the collecting officers.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the Municipal Budget Officer to fund for honoraria of BACs, TWGs and Head Secretariat from 1) Collections from successfully completed procurement projects limited, however, to activities prior to awarding of contracts to winning bidders: a.) Proceeds from sale of bid documents, b.) Fees from contractor/supplier registry, c.) Fees charged for copies of minutes of bid openings, BAC Resolutions and other BAC documents, d.) Protest fees; and e.) Proceeds from bid security forfeiture. 2) In the case of LGUs, savings from the local budgets approved by their respective Sanggunian subject to the pertinent provisions of the Local Government Code of 1991 (RA 7160).
observations.managementAction: Starting CY 2016, the sourced is the proceeds of the bid documents and not from the regular budget. For CY 2016, the BAC as compassion, did not claim honorarium.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality to implement the provisions of COA Circular 2007-001 and ensure proper granting, utilization and liquidation of cash fund transfers. The Bids and Award Committee to select an NGO by bidding. Municipal Budget to submit projects/ programs in line with the specific guidelines provided by DILG-DOE Circular No. 98-01 at the beginning of the year to serve as guide and reference in the release of funds charged to shares from national wealth. And to ensure that projects and programs to be implemented should be in accordance with the prescribed guidelines to avoid misuse and misapplication of the subject funds.
observations.managementAction: Management had already informed the NGO for submission.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the management for the creation of the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board in accordance with the provision of law; The plan should be reviewed and updated as required by law to ensure its sustainability, relevance and effectiveness; Campaigns should be collaborated with the barangay officials concerned; Continued and stricter implementation of waste segregation, and thorough collection of waste based on set schedules; and Convert the open dumpsite to a controlled dump site.
observations.managementAction: The management informed the audit team that they have already submitted the solid waste management plan to the NSWMC.
